preprocessing_steps: Load the CSV file into a pandas DataFrame.
Extract unique product SKUs to form the index set I.
Create dictionaries mapping each product SKU to its corresponding parameters (price, cost, molding time, assembly time, max capacity).
Define global constants for total molding and assembly capacities.

parameters.description: Sale price per unit for each product
Material cost per unit for each product
Molding minutes required per unit for each product
Assembly minutes required per unit for each product
Maximum market capacity for each product

parameters.python_representation: C_m = 5000  # Example value, typically provided as a global constraint
C_a = 4000  # Example value, typically provided as a global constraint

mapping_explanation: Product_SKU maps to the index set I.
C_m and C_a are global constants representing total resource capacities.
assumptions: Each row in the CSV represents a unique product SKU.
Global capacities C_m and C_a are not in the CSV and must be provided separately as constants.
Data contains no missing values for the mapped columns.
full_script:
import pandas as pd

# Load data
df = pd.read_csv('optimization_pipeline_test_medium.csv')

# Define index set I
I = df['Product_SKU'].unique()

# Define parameters as dictionaries keyed by product SKU
p = dict(zip(df['Product_SKU'], df['Sale_Price_Per_Unit']))
c = dict(zip(df['Product_SKU'], df['Material_Cost_Per_Unit']))
m = dict(zip(df['Product_SKU'], df['Molding_Mins_Req']))
a = dict(zip(df['Product_SKU'], df['Assembly_Mins_Req']))
M = dict(zip(df['Product_SKU'], df['Max_Market_Capacity']))

# Global constants for resource capacities
C_m = 5000  # Total available molding minutes
C_a = 4000  # Total available assembly minutes

# Data structures are now ready for optimization modeling
